description: §8.3 / §52.1 two server-fns over two DISTINCT seeded tables (RUNTIME, serverDb driver) — loadUsers reads `FROM users`, loadPosts reads `FROM posts`; each hydrates its own client cell (@users ← 2 rows, @posts ← 3 rows) from its own seeded table. Proves the serverDb driver keys rows by table and dispatches two independent routes. NO JOIN (the SQL stub cannot join — two separate single-table reads).
rationale: §12.2 / §52.1: two functions each touch a server resource and escalate independently; two clicks round-trip through two distinct emitted routes. §8.3: each `.all()` resolves to Row[] for its own table. The serverDb driver seeds `users` and `posts` separately, and `_scrml_sql` keys the returned rows by the `FROM <table>` extracted from each query — so @users hydrates the 2 seeded users and @posts the 3 seeded posts, independently. Asserted purely as data-flow across two tables — deliberately NO JOIN / cross-table query (the BLOCKED engine-semantics surface; the stub returns each whole table).
